body.title: [triage] Migrate decisions/ readers to designs/ and retire the emit_design decision projection
body.intention: I intend that the design artifact is the single home its readers point at, so the transitional back-compat decision projection can be deleted and the -1-decision delta of the boundary contract actually completes.
body.description:
FOLLOW-ON to T-design-first-artifact-type. That keystone made `design` first-class but, to avoid stranding the broad decisions/ reader surface, emit_design PROJECTS a derived D-* decision into decisions/ (design=source of truth, projection=read-model). This ticket retires that projection by pointing the readers at designs/ (via design_store.iter_designs/get_design or a projected view), then removing the project_decision default.

**Reader surface to migrate (grep 'decisions/'):** skills/context-load/run (Step 2a/3 recent+active decisions), devlab/claudecode/validity_sweep.py, decision-rollup/auto-close path (cc_queue.py / outcome_check.py), unseen_university/devices/web_server/server.py, igor cognition readers (bliss_integrator, self_inspect, resource_manager, pursuits, cortex). Coordinate with the audit re-key (T-rekey-decision-first-skills-to-design-first) which handles audit-* + /outcome + /weekly-retro.

**Design rules:** one home no exceptions (CLAUDE.md #1); homogeneity over special-case; delete old copies after successful migration (no dups).
**Scope boundary:** IN — re-point the non-audit decisions/ readers at designs/ + drop the projection default in design_emit. OUT — the audit re-key (its own ticket); OUT — deleting historical decisions/ records (they stay readable).
**Test plan:** proof = a reader (e.g. context-load recent-decisions or validity_sweep) surfaces a design emitted with project_decision=False (red->green: reader finds the design without a projected D-*). 
**Completion criteria:** the named readers read designs/; design_emit no longer projects by default; boundary-contract -1-decision delta complete; reconciled to architecture/workflow-levels.

Related: T-design-first-artifact-type, T-rekey-decision-first-skills-to-design-first, architecture/cc.0.workflow-levels.*, note reader-gap-decision 2026-07-10.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
